name: Butterscotch-Heath Bar Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
1. In a mixing bowl, cream together the oil, white and brown sugars until well blended.
2. Stir in the vanilla and butterscotch extracts.
3. Blend in the eggs.
4. Mix together the flour, baking powder, and salt.
5. Fold the dry mixture into the batter.
6. Fold in the Heath bar pieces and pecans.
7. Place the batter on the prepared baking sheet. Form it into two logs, each about 10 to 12 inches long and 2 1/2 to 3 1/2 inches in diameter.
8. Bake in the preheated oven for about 25 minutes until set.
9. Allow the biscuits to cool thoroughly.
Baking Trick: For ultra-thin biscotti that are easily cut, after the first baking, wrap each log of baked biscotti and freeze overnight. The next day, slice as thin as desired and return to the baking sheet. Bake for about 12 minutes on one side, then turn over and bake for an additional 5 minutes on the other side. Allow to cool well.
Any brand of chocolate-covered brittle or toffee bar can be used. Coarse pieces work better than "Heath Bar Chips", which are too small. (36 to 48 biscotti)
